event.name: FLEET_OG_IRON_ELITE
event.text: The Rebel fleet has taken control of this beacon, but they have been met with opposition from Iron Watch forces. As the ships rage on in combat in the distance, an Elite ship breaks off to fight you.

choice.text: (Adv. Engines) Try to escape the Elite.

event.name: FLEET_OG_IRON_ACE
event.text: The Rebel fleet has taken control of this beacon, but they have been met with opposition from Iron Watch forces. As the ships rage on in combat in the distance, an MFK Ace ship breaks off to fight you.

choice.text: (Adv. Engines) Try to escape the Elite.

event.name: GUARD_EXECUTOR_OG_IRON_DESTROY
event.text: You have defeated the Iron Watch guard. Whatever might have been in their cargo has for certain been destroyed, but you can at least salvage some supplies from the ship.

event.name: GUARD_EXECUTOR_OG_IRON_DEADCREW
event.text: You have defeated the Iron Watch guard. Without crew on board to stop you, you can investigate their cargo. You find a weapon to bring back to the ship, and move on to scrap their hull.

event.name: GUARD_EXECUTOR_OG_IRON_FOLLOWUP
event.text: By killing the Iron Watch guard, you have no doubt caused them to dispatch reinforcements to track you down. Watch your step as you explore the sector.

event.name: START_OG_NEUTRON
event.text:
The arrival beacon lands you in the middle of the Iron Watch's fleet. The fleet pays no attention to you, you observe most ships are preoccupied with preparing for the approaching rebel fleet, engineering ships attend to a number of vessels undergoing repairs.
Your sensors pickup a massive energy signature in the sector, a neutron star, visible even from here. You're unsure how the iron watch have managed to survive with a thing like that around, if you get caught in it's beam it's sure to rip your ship apart.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_START_IRON
event.text:
You're directed through a series of disinterested officers until you're finally face to face with Admiral Micari, the same Admiral who personally requested your help.
"Captain, glad you made it, As you may know, I'm Admiral Micari of the 4th Defence Fleet. I'll spare the rest of the formalities and cut right to the chase. When I originally requested your assistance, the intention was to have you fight alongside the fleet. The Rebel fleet has already taken out several of our forward outposts, frankly, we could really use another capable ship like yours on the frontline."

"That plan has changed, two hours ago we received a distress call coming our research base, the one positioned right next to the neutron star you may have noticed in the sector. The signal cut out before we could get a clear read on what was happening, it might just be equipment failure, but I fear the worst."
"I can't pull any of my ships from the front line to follow up on the distress signal, not with the Rebel fleet so close. But that research base is part of why we're even here in the first place, I can't disclose details but it's important, and so I can't just ignore the distress call."

"That's where you come in, Captain. I need you to investigate the distress signal and make sure the base is alright. If there is time after that is dealt with, we'd appreciate any help you can provide to the frontline, but that base is the top priority."
"Good luck out there Captain."

event.name: GUARD_OG_NEUTRON_FRIENDLY_SUPPORT
event.text: An Officer puts you on a secure line with one of the nearby support ships, who awaits your command.
Several repair drones are dispatched to repair you.
A refuelling drone is dispatched to refuel your ship.
A resupply drone is dispatched to resupply your ship.
A resupply drone is dispatched to resupply your ship.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_START_DAWN
event.text:
Before you're able to interact with any of the Iron Watch fleet, you're contacted by a New Dawn ship hidden inside a nearby asteroid field.
"Excellent, you made it here and the Iron Watch don't suspect a thing. As you may remember, this sector is home to that neutron star your sensor may detect in the distance. The Iron Watch didn't establish the research base here, instead this sector was incorporated as the Neutron Star Exclusion Zone when the original scientists joined the Iron Watch."

"Their work here has resulted in many new technologies, you may have noticed that many of the Iron Watch shields are equipped with shielding designed to protect them from the radiation of the neutron star. This is what we're after, we've managed to steal or loot many copies of the shielding device from Iron Watch ships, but its not enough to equip more than our intelligence operatives, we need the schematics and designs for the technology."

"This is where you come in, we believe that the schematics for this technology are contained on original research station in orbit around the neutron star. Our intelligence operatives have prepared their ship for a raid on the station, but with the rebel fleet on the way they've stepped up the protection around the facility. We need you to provide the distraction that lets our intelligence operatives get close enough to the facility to initiate the raid."

"Excellent, I'll upload the location of the research base, not that it's hard to find being in orbit of the Neutron Star. Our operatives will be ready to move in once you distract the guards, good luck."
"Well the raid is happening regardless of your participation, I'll upload the location of the research base, not that it's hard to find. If you show up, we'll assume you're onboard."

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: Location of the Iron Watch Research Station.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_HAZARD_RESEARCH_STATION_RETURN
event.text: You have a moment to do something onboard your ship.

destroyed.text: One of the turrets is launched off into empty space as the ship explodes, throwing valuable debris in every direction.

deadCrew.text: "You will regret this! Karma always comes back to people like you. Damn you Renegades... the New Dawn will..." They die before they can finish this last remark.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_RESEARCH_STATION_IRON
event.text:
The research station hangs in orbit above the neutron star, close enough that the radiation in unavoidable, you'll want to avoid spending too long here.
As you bring your ship alongside the station and begin docking procedures, its immediately clear something's gone wrong, the hull of the station is scored with damage, half the solar panels are completely missing.

The airlock opens into the station, thankfully these part has stayed pressurised despite the damage. You're immediately face to face with a figuring in the white coat of a scientist. "You're from the Iron Watch? Close enough, come in quickly."

You're ushered towards a nearby meeting room, a large screen is cycling through various images of dangerous looking ship, stealth in design, it must be what attacked the station.
"We were attacked, that ship," they gesture to the one on the screen, "is unlike anything we've seen before. We've heard stories of 'ghost' ships like this but no one had seriously considered them. We barely saw it coming, it came from the direction of the neutron star, shielded from our sensors by the radiation. They were shielded by one of our own Synchrotron Barriers, developed here on this station to shield our ships against the radiation of the neutron star."

Alarms blaring across the station, the screen on the wall switches to show a new ship, painted in New Dawn colours. With the station as defenceless as it is, you're have the only thing capable of preventing the New Dawn vessel from finishing the station off. You rush back to your ship and disengage from the station, now clear of the station's protection you'll have to survive both the enemy and the force of the radiation.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_RESEARCH_STATION_SAVED
event.text:
You quickly return to the safety of the station's Synchrotron Barrier. Docking with the station, you head back to the meeting room with the scientist, who is now pacing back and forth across the room.
"That won't be the last of them. The stealth ship, 'ghost' ship as some have been calling it, it wasn't just here to destroy us. No, it was here to steal our stock of Synchrotron Barriers, but worse, they've stolen the schematics, previously they've only been able to get their hands on the devices by stealing them off of our own ships, but now they could begin manufacturing the devices themselves."

"The good news however is that we began placing tracking devices in the barriers, we should be able to track the ones stolen by the 'ghost' ship itself, and if we're quick enough we may be able to prevent the schematics from escaping the sector."
"I'm humbly requesting your help here, we need to recover the schematics. I hope it's not too much to ask, but I'd like to come with you, I assure you I'll be no hindrance onboard the ship."

"I realise I forgot to introduce myself. Dr. Silas at your service." "There's one more thing, this station is done, it's in no condition to be repaired this close to the neutron star. If you can get everyone onboard to safety we can transfer the only Synchrotron Barrier remaining to your ship, the one currently hooked up to the station."
You're able to load the remaining station staff onto your ship, before Dr. Silas has the Synchrotron Barrier carefully moved onto your ship, and integrated into your engines system.
You're able to deliver the station's staff to a nearby outpost, as you leave you watch the station disintegrate under the full force of the radiation.

"I will stay with the rest of the station's staff then, good luck with the rest of the mission then, Captain." "There's one more thing, this station is done, it's in no condition to be repaired this close to the neutron star. If you can get everyone onboard to safety we can transfer the only Synchrotron Barrier remaining to your ship, the one currently hooked up to the station."
You're able to load the remaining station staff onto your ship, before the scientist has the Synchrotron Barrier carefully moved onto your ship, and integrated into your engines system.
You're able to deliver the station's staff to a nearby outpost, as you leave you watch the station disintegrate under the full force of the radiation.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_GHOST_IRON_QUEST

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: The Location of the 'Ghost' ship with the stolen Synchrotron Barriers.

event.text: You arrive face to face with 'Ghost' ship, it's even more dangerous looking in person. Sleek and armed to the teeth, this is sure to be a tough fight. You're given no chance to avoid the fight as you're immediately within range of their weapons.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_GHOST_DAWN_QUEST

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: The Location of the Midnight Sun vessel which betrayed you.

event.text: You arrive face to face with Midnight Sun ship. Sleek and armed to the teeth, this is sure to be a tough fight. You're given no chance to avoid the fight as you're immediately within range of their weapons.

event.name: SHIP_OG_MIDNIGHT_HUNTER_MIDFIGHT_RESET
event.text:
The enemy vessel engages it's emergency thrusters and flies directly into the pulsar, as you turn to chase the pulsar launches a mass of magnetised plasma and solar particles into your path, preventing you from following.
Your computers calculate that based on the trajectory they were on, you have about 30 seconds before the ship will come back around to your side again.

triggeredEventBox.tooltip: The 'Ghost' ship will come around the pulsar and back into combat range in this time!

event.text: The last of the crew finally fall dead, actually dead this time. A number of sections within the ship are locked down and armoured, whatever they contain is likely to be important.

event.name: SHIP_OG_MIDNIGHT_HUNTER_DEADCREW_RESET
event.text: Just as you thought you were done, a serious of panels in the enemy vessel's cockpit pop open, ungracefully dumping a number of cloned soldiers onto the deck.

event.name: SHIP_OG_MIDNIGHT_HUNTER_RESUME
event.text: The battle resumes, however something is different this time, it appears they had some kind of super weapon hidden in the bowls of their ship, and its finally online!

event.name: SHIP_OG_MIDNIGHT_HUNTER_DESTROYED
event.text: The vessel explodes flinging debris everywhere, your sensors pickup a number of armoured sections which hold together under the explosion, whatever they contain is likely to be salvageable.

event.name: SHIP_OG_MIDNIGHT_HUNTER_SALVAGE_SILAS
event.text: You search the armoured sections, most of them don't contain anything of note, however one of them contains a data bank. Dr. Silas inspects it, "Aha, this contains the schematics for the Synchrotron Barrier, it also has the schematic for the ship itself, I'll upload that to your ship then destroy the barrier's schematic." It only takes a moment for the drive to start screeching, a moment later it stops, broken.

event.name: SHIP_OG_MIDNIGHT_HUNTER_SALVAGE
event.text: You search the armoured sections, most of them don't contain anything of note, however one of them contains a data bank, a quick inspection reveals both the schematic for the Synchrotron Barrier, as well as the schematic for the ship itself, you copy both over to your own ship before destroying the data bank.

event.name: SHIP_OG_MIDNIGHT_HUNTER_WEAPON
event.text: Eventually you stumble upon what appears to be the core of the artillery weapon, you're able to extract it from it's mangled armour, it appears to be partially functional, but you'll need the parts from a powerful weapon, and a turret slot to equip it in.

destroyed.text: One of the turrets is launched off into empty space as the ship explodes, throwing valuable debris in every direction.

deadCrew.text: The last of the station's armed personal fall, dead. You return to your ship and await communication from the Midnight Sun.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_RESEARCH_STATION_DAWN
event.text:
The research station hangs in orbit above the neutron star, close enough that the radiation in unavoidable, you'll want to avoid spending too long here.
The station appears well defended, with a few turrets and weapons mounted, additionally it appears to be protected from the radiation by some sort of strange shield, mostly purple but burning bright blue where the radiation is strongest.

Not a moment later your comms crackle with an incoming signal. The signal is a tight-beam, encrypted, you're sensors aren't able to locate it's source.
"So you're who they sent, I hope you're good enough. But enough with the pleasantries..." A ship uncloaks itself almost on top of you, the source of the signal, painted almost entirely black, the only source of colour a tiny golden sun insignia near the cockpit.

"...We're New Dawn Intelligence, or the Midnight Sun as some may know us. We will be raiding the station, but we require you to provide the distraction so that we may slip close enough to secure our prize, the Synchrotron Schematics."
"When you attack, disable their defences if you can, but it is vitally important that you do not destroy the station. To ensure an efficient mission we'll be sending over one of our own crew to oversee your side of the operation."

The Midnight Sun Operative arrives via short range teleporter. After a quick inspection of your ship they take position in the piloting room to oversee the attack.
It's not more than a few moments later that the operative receives an encrypted message, they turn to you, "We're ready to go Captain, we'll move on your mark."

triggeredEventBox.tooltip: Survive in combat with the station until this time, ensure the station is not destroyed!

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_RESEARCH_STATION_FAILED
event.text: "We're severely disappointed in you Captain. Do not contact us again." With that the Midnight Sun retrieve their crew and jump away.
Perhaps you should have been more careful to not destroy the station.

destroyed.text: One of the turrets is launched off into empty space as the ship explodes, throwing valuable debris in every direction.

deadCrew.text: "You will regret this! Karma always comes back to people like you. Damn you Renegades... the New Dawn will..." They die before they can finish this last remark.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_DAWN_BARRIER_GET
event.text: Amongst the remains of the ship you find, intact, the Synchrotron Barrier. After consulting scans of the device you're able to install it on your own ship.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_RESEARCH_STATION_WON
event.text:
Your sensors detect the Midnight Sun ship disengage from the station, it quickly retreats to safety, it appears that your job here is done. You begin disengaging from the station yourself.
The Midnight Sun operative rushes over to you, "What are you doing Captain? I haven't received the signal, we shouldn't disengage yet."

choice.text: Show them that the Midnight Sun ship has already disengaged to safety.

event.text: "But then why hav-" They cut off, you cannot see their face beneath the helmet, but their body language gives you the impression that something that something is very wrong. They appear to be interacting with some sort of internal system within their armour.

choice.event:
The Midnight Sun vessel begins charging it's jump drive, "We hoped that the station would do the job for us, you unfortunately exceeded our expectations Captain. Good bye."\ lightspeed
Continue...
Not a moment later a new ship uncloaks, this one a more traditional New Dawn vessel. One of your crew turn to you, "Captain, that ship, they're refusing our hails, scans indicate they're bringing their weapons online."
The operative has gone very still beside you. When they finally speak, their voice is quiet. "This wasn't apart of any briefing I received."
Continue...
The operative turns to face you, now slightly slumped, apparently given up on whatever they were trying to do before. "I was told this was a clean extraction. Get in, coordinate, get out. That we would deal with you later, whether that was buying your silence, or silencing you ourselves I do not know. Apparently there were other plans, plans I wasn't aware of. I should have known it wouldn't be this simple."
Continue...
"I know where the Midnight Sun ship will have gone. There's a small Midnight Sun outpost nearby, that's where they'll be offloading the barriers and schematic. If you desire revenge, as I do, that's where we'll need to go."
"I'd like to join your crew officially, it's clear that the Midnight Sun is done with me, and you're my best bet for revenge."
Prepare for combat.

textList.name: TEXT_DESTROYED_OG_MIDNIGHT
textList.text: The Midnight Sun captain lets out one final expletive as the ship is obliterated.
The hull of the Midnight Sun vessel is dispersed out into space. You swoop in to salvage the remains.
The Midnight Sun ship explodes, extinguishing all lives within. Your crew avoids their remains as they salvage the ship pieces.
One of the turrets is launched off into empty space as the ship explodes, throwing valuable debris in every direction.

textList.name: TEXT_DEAD_CREW_OG_MIDNIGHT
textList.text: "Your bloodlust... will be your downfall someday, Renegade. You are only proof... the Federation... is no better than the Rebellion..."
Without a word, the Midnight Sun captain falls to the ground, gasping for air as the life is extinguished from their eyes. You salvage the hull of their ship.
"You will regret this! Karma always comes back to people like you. Damn you Renegades..."

textList.name: TEXT_SURRENDER_OG_MIDNIGHT
textList.text: "Alright, you win! Here's some equipment from our stores. Leave us alone!"
The ship repeatedly hails you. It looks like they want to surrender.
"You are considerably more well-armed than I would have thought. We surrender."
They send you a message: "Your ship is surprisingly well equipped! Please take this and let us live."
"Please don't kill us. We will give you everything we have!"
They offer you some of their cargo if you let them live.
They offer to give you some of their goods if you don't destroy their ship.
You get a frantic message, "Take everything we have. Just don't kill us!"
"You have clearly bested us. We merely request our lives."
"You have made your point, we are beaten. Take these and leave us to our shame."
"Take everything we have and let's both forget this happened."

event.name: ESCAPE_OG_MIDNIGHT
event.text: Scans show that the Midnight Sun ship is trying to escape. Destroy it before it can get away!

textList.name: TEXT_GOTAWAY_OG_MIDNIGHT
textList.text: The Midnight Sun vessel has slipped from your grasp, abandoning you at the beacon.
Your attempts at destroying the Midnight Sun vessel have failed, and the ship has escaped.

event.name: GOTAWAY_OG_MIDNIGHT_REMAINS
event.text: The Midnight Sun vessel may have escaped, but not everything has gone wrong. A trail of scrap from the wreckage has been left behind, which you collect.

escape.text: They look like they don't want to fight. They are trying to escape.

event.name: TRANSPORT_OG_MIDNIGHT_LOOT

beaconType.visitedTooltip: Explored location. Nothing left of interest.

event.text: This beacon hosts a Midnight Sun transport. It's safe to bet the ship is most likely carrying some special tech. If you can kill its crew, their cargo will be yours.

choice.text: Demand the surrender of their goods.

event.text: You prepare to secure their cargo by force.
They stay outside your weapons range, and eventually jump away.

event.name: TRANSPORT_CAPTURE_OG_MIDNIGHT
event.text: The crew is dead, leaving you with the ship. Its cargo is yours for the taking. Aboard is the special tech you expected, which you bring back to your ship.

event.name: STOREVENT_OG_NEUTRON_PLANET
event.text: One of the few settlements in the system, this planet is protected by an incredibly dense atmosphere which mitigates some of the effects of the radiation. The store is located on the surface of the planet.
The dense atmosphere means it'll take a moment to get down to the surface, travelling here in the middle of the Neutron Storm could be difficult.

triggeredEventBox.tooltip: Time until you arrive on the surface of the planet!

event.text: You prepare for your next jump.

event.name: STOREVENT_OG_NEUTRON_PLANET_ARRIVE
event.text: You finally arrive on the surface of the planet, you take a moment to rest before heading to the store.

event.name: STOREVENT_OG_NEUTRON_MIDNIGHT_ATTACK
event.text: The distress beacon appears to be originating from a small, Iron Watch outpost under attack from a strange stealth ship!
You prepare for combat.
Fortunately the ship remain oblivious to your presence.

event.name: STOREVENT_OG_NEUTRON_MIDNIGHT_WIN
event.text: You have rescued the store from their attackers. The store owners offer to trade.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_RESCUE_CIVILIAN
event.text: A small transport is adrift at this beacon, sensors indicate that the ship is battered and running on the absolute minimum power to keep the 2 occupants alive, huddled together at the back of the ship.

The two humans inside are visibly shaken, half starved and frozen. They explain that they barely survived getting stranded in the middle of the neutron storm as it passed through the area.
They're grateful for your help and offer to reward you once you deliver them to somewhere safe, like the exit beacon or a store.
You leave the ship and it's crew helplessly drifting. Their sensors, along with most of the rest of their ship, were offline, so at least they won't know how close they came to being saved.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_HAZARD_ASTEROID_FIELD
event.text:
This beacon has unfortunately been placed right up against a large pulsar, and to make matters worse it's also in the middle of an asteroid field.
The only upside is that because of how dangerous this place is, no one has been able to extract the valuable resources in this asteroid field.

choice.text: (Mining Equipment - 15s) Attempt to mine the asteroids.
(Mining Drone - 15s) Attempt to mine the asteroids. [-1|]

event.text: You launch your mining drone to begin cutting away at the rock faces your ship can't reach.

choice.text: (Scrap Recovery Arm - 30s) Attempt to mine the asteroids.
(Beam Drone - 30s) Attempt to mine the asteroids. [-1|]

event.text: You launch your beam drone to begin cutting away at the rock faces your ship can't reach.

choice.text: (Damaging Beam Weapon - 30s) Attempt to mine the asteroids. [-1|]
(Magnetic Arm - 45s) Attempt to mine the asteroids.
(Engineering Crew - 45s) Attempt to mine the asteroids. [-1|]
(No Equipment - 60s) Attempt to mine the asteroids.

event.text: You make preparations to jump as soon as possible.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_ASTEROID_FIELD_START_60
event.text: You begin cutting into some nearby asteroids where your sensors indicate useful minerals.

triggeredEventBox.tooltip: Time until you'll have extracted the minerals from the asteroid.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_ASTEROID_FIELD_START_45
event.text: You begin cutting into some nearby asteroids where your sensors indicate useful minerals.

triggeredEventBox.tooltip: Time until you'll have extracted the minerals from the asteroid.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_ASTEROID_FIELD_START_30
event.text: You begin cutting into some nearby asteroids where your sensors indicate useful minerals.

triggeredEventBox.tooltip: Time until you'll have extracted the minerals from the asteroid.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_ASTEROID_FIELD_START_15
event.text: You begin cutting into some nearby asteroids where your sensors indicate useful minerals.

triggeredEventBox.tooltip: Time until you'll have extracted the minerals from the asteroid.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_ASTEROID_FIELD_FINISH
event.text: You carefully extract as much usable material as possible from the nearest asteroids.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_DAWN_WRECK
event.text: You've come across the wreck of a strange ship. It appears to be one the New Dawn ships you've heard of around here.
Before you're able to investigate anything the ship roars to life, your scans now indicate that it's under control of Ghosts!
You avoid the ship and prepare for your next jump.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_DISTRESS_CIVILIAN
event.text: You arrive at the beacon to discover the source of the distress beacon is a civilian shuttle, and the source of their distress, a New Dawn ship bearing down on them, weapons hot.
You power on your weapons and move in to attack.
You avoid the ship and prepare for your next jump.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_DISTRESS_CIVILIAN_FOLLOWUP
event.text:
You turn your attention back to the civilian shuttle, it isn't hard to located them. The shuttle is heavily damaged, far beyond repair, especially out here in the middle of nowhere.
The inhabitants of the shuttle, a pair of humans request that you deliver them to the nearest safe zone, they promise to reward you fairly upon arrival.
They come aboard and fervently promise to try and stay out of the way and out of trouble.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_DISTRESS_PIRATE
event.text:
To your dismay, the distress call emanates from a clearly marked pirate vessel. Scans indicate they're damaged, not beyond repair, but significant enough to be a problem.
They immediately contact you on the open channels, probably trying to get ahead of you activating your weapons. "Listen Captain, I won't play games with ya, I know what we look like, an easy target, right?"

"We got caught in that big star's beam, the newton star or whatever they call it, really screwed us up, but we have something worth your while a genuwine piece of New Dawn technology! We couldn't work out how to use it ourselves, but I'm sure its valuable, it's the reason we got stuck out here after all."

choice.text: Buy the "technology" in exchange for the parts they need to repair their FTL drive. [35~, 3|]

event.text: You offload the parts to them, they send over the "technology" which your computer is able to identify as a turret pre-energiser.
You leave the ship to fend for itself and prepare for your next jump.
You leave the ship to fend for itself and prepare for your next jump.

event.name: OG_NEUTRON_DISTRESS_PIRATE_ATTACK
event.text: You power on your weapons and move in to attack.

event.name: OG_IRON_DELIVERY
event.text: You've arrived face to face with an Iron Watch transport, your sensors are able to detect their jump drive being charged.
As they execute the jump, you watch an undefended asteroid slam into the side of the ship, tearing a section free. This doesn't impede the jump and the transport disappears a moment later.

Most of the wreckage is useless, but you do eventually find a cargo container, and inside an unusual looking turret. It appears that this turret had a specific destination, an outpost nearby. You note down the coordinates.

event.name: OG_IRON_DELIVERY_OUTPOST

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: The intended destination of the turret you found.

event.text: This beacon is home to a large outpost, several ships can be seen in and around the outpost. You spot the damaged transport nearby.

You exit your ship and immediately overhear the loud conversation happening nearby "I don't know what we're going to do, they're going to take away my ship over this. Why did we ever go into that asteroid field, it didn't even save us more than like 1 jump."

"Oh thank goodness, how in the multiverse did you find it?" You explain that you saw them get hit and lose it. "You're a lifesaver, here take these supplies as a reward." With the turret in tow, the captain of the transport heads off into the outpost.

choice.text: You don't have the missing turret with you.

event.text: You prepare for your next jump.

choice.text: You can dock at the outpost but without crew it will be hard to do anything there.

event.text: You prepare for your next jump.

event.name: OG_IRON_DAWN_HIDEOUT
event.text: You arrive at the beacon face to face with a New Dawn scout. You immediately detect the ship powering up it's jump drive, you only have a moment before they jump away.

choice.text: Attempt to fire on their jump drive!

event.text: The ship doesn't immediately jump away, it appears you have their attention. You explain that you're not going to kill them, you just want to know what they're doing here. They don't immediately respond and they keep their jump drive ready.

Eventually they respond, "Renegade, if you want to know anything you're going to have to prove yourself to us. There's a nearby Iron Watch supply depot, go there and take it out, then we'll talk." With that they jump away.
The ship immediately shuts off their comms, a moment later they're gone. It appears that they have no interest in hearing you out, perhaps if they had heard of you they'd be more willing to listen.
The ship jumps away, you prepare for your next jump.
The New Dawn scout easily dodges your attack, a moment later they've jumped away.
You score a direct hit on their engines, disabling their jump drive.

event.name: OG_IRON_DAWN_HIDEOUT_QUEST

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: Location of the Iron Watch supply depot you've been tasked with destroying by the New Dawn.

event.text: You've arrived at beacon to find the Iron Watch supply depot, unfortunately it's not alone, an Iron Watch ship stands on guard nearby.
The Iron Watch vessel quickly moves in between you and the depot, prepare to fight.
The Iron Watch are very grateful for the warning and they offer some supplies as thanks.
You keep your distance and prepare for your next jump.

With the guard ship dealt with you're able to access, loot and destroy the depot. It appears that the New Dawn were watching because it isn't very long before you receive a message. "It appears you've already met with some other members of the New Dawn, We'll find you at the New Dawn forward operating base."

event.name: OG_IRON_DAWN_HIDEOUT_QUEST_WIN_INITIATE
event.text: With the guard ship dealt with you're able to access, loot and destroy the depot. It appears that the New Dawn were watching because it isn't very long before you receive a message. "Find us at the New Dawn forward operating base, we'll talk there."

textList.name: TEXT_ENCOUNTER_OG_IRON
textList.text: As you jump into the system a rogue Iron Watch ship advances on your position. They are refusing all hails. Prepare for a fight.
Soon after arriving in the system you are hailed by a small cruiser. "What good fortune that we happen to run into each other. Nothing personal, but you have some information we need!"
The only other ship at this beacon messages you: "Finally, after months of waiting, someone has fallen into our trap!"
You barely have time to register jump completion before your ship warns you of an incoming ship with weapons hot.

event.name: ENCOUNTER_OG_IRON_PULSAR
event.text: This beacon has been placed too close to a neutron star! The radiation field will temporarily ionise your systems. Iron Watch ships have apparently been designed to remain quite effective in the presence of pulsars, you're in for a bad time.

event.name: ENCOUNTER_OG_PIRATE_PULSAR
event.text: This beacon has been placed too close to a neutron star! The radiation field will temporarily ionise your systems. A pirate, apparently oblivious to the danger of the pulsar, moves in to engage.

event.name: OG_IRON_PROTOTYPE
event.text: You've arrived at a rather desolate beacon, there is very little of note here apart from the Iron Watch outpost you detect in orbit around a nearby planet.
Just as you were getting bored another ship jumps into the system, its unlike any Iron Watch ship you've seen before, it appears to be some type of new cruiser, possibly a prototype.
You watch as the prototype docks with the distant outpost. It's only a few minutes later when the ship undocks and jumps away before you can do anything.

choice.text: (Positive Reputation) Inquire about the ship with the outpost.
(Neutral Reputation) Inquire about the ship with the outpost.
(Negative Reputation) Inquire about the ship with the outpost.
(Adv. Sensors) Scan the jump wake created by the ship.

event.text: By scanning the radiation emitted by the jump you're able to triangulate the approximate destination for the prototype.
You keep your distance and prepare for your next jump.

event.name: OG_IRON_PROTOTYPE_OUTPOST_FRIENDLY
event.text: It's a moment before the the outpost greets you, "We're not really supposed to give out this information, but we'll make an exception for you."

event.name: OG_IRON_PROTOTYPE_OUTPOST
event.text: It's a moment before the the outpost greets you, and when they do you're met with an immediate wall, "I'm sorry, we can't provide any information on that ship."
The outpost receives your scrap, "While your donation is very kind, we still can't tell you anything about that ship."

The outpost receives your scrap, "Now that's something, we can't tell you, but recently our communications have actually been malfunctioning and sending secure information on public channels, it really needs to be looked into..." A moment later your crew pickups the location of a ship being broadcast on one of the public channels.

choice.text: Threaten the outpost for the information.

event.text: The outpost is effectively defenceless against your weapons, they quickly capitulate to your demands, uploading the data on the prototype. They also kindly return your donation.
You move away from the outpost and prepare for your next jump.

The outpost receives your scrap, "Now that's something, we can't tell you, but recently our communications have actually been malfunctioning and sending secure information on public channels, it really needs to be looked into..." A moment later your crew pickups the location of a ship being broadcast on one of the public channels.

choice.text: Threaten the outpost for the information.

event.text: The outpost is effectively defenceless against your weapons, they quickly capitulate to your demands, uploading the data on the prototype.

While you make small talk with the outpost, you configure the Hacking system to get a read of their data storage, and download their recent communications data. Once the download is complete, you bid farewell with the location of the prototype in hand. Unfortunately your hack is detected just as you're leaving.

choice.text: (Adv. Hacking) Remotely access their data storage.

event.text: While you make small talk with the outpost, you configure the Hacking system to get a read of their data storage, and download their recent communications data. Once the download is complete, you bid farewell with the location of the prototype in hand.
You move away from the outpost and prepare for your next jump.

event.name: OG_IRON_PROTOTYPE_OUTPOST_CRIMINAL
event.text: It's a moment before the the outpost greets you, and when they do you're met with an immediate wall, "Even if we could tell you we wouldn't."

choice.text: Threaten the outpost for the information.

event.text: The outpost is effectively defenceless against your weapons, they quickly capitulate to your demands, uploading the data on the prototype.

You configure the Hacking system to get a read of their data storage, and download their recent communications data. Once the download is complete, you bid farewell with the location of the prototype in hand. Unfortunately your hack is detected just as you're leaving and you detect them reporting it to nearby Iron Watch outposts.

choice.text: (Adv. Hacking) Remotely access their data storage.

event.text: You configure the Hacking system to get a read of their data storage, and download their recent communications data. Once the download is complete, you bid farewell with the location of the prototype in hand.
You move away from the outpost and prepare for your next jump.

event.name: OG_IRON_PROTOTYPE_DESTINATION

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: Location of the prototype Iron Watch vessel.

event.text: You arrive at the beacon to discover the Iron Watch vessel in orbit around the pulsar here, it appears they're testing their weapon systems on a nearby asteroid.

As you watch a large wave from the pulsar sweeps across the prototype, your sensors indicate several of the systems onboard are immediately disabled by the pulsar. If that's not bad enough a new ship reveals itself from behind a nearby asteroid, It appears to be one of those New Dawn ships, and it's moving in on the prototype.

choice.text: Move in and defend the Iron Watch prototype.
Aid the New Dawn ship in it's attack on the heavily armed prototype.

event.text: You turn you attention to the prototype, unfortunately by the time you're ready the ship has almost fully recovered from the pulsar hit. You're not alone though, you have the support of the New Dawn ship.

choice.text: Retreat to a safe distance and stay out of the fight.

event.text: You move away from the two ships and prepare for your next jump.

event.name: OG_IRON_PROTOTYPE_WIN
event.text: The Iron Watch ship has mostly recovered from the attack, they contact you. "We're grateful for your help Captain, we're not sure we would have survived that without you help. Perhaps it's time we took a more active presence in your war with the rebellion. We'll send the blueprints for the prototype along to the federation, perhaps they'll be able to use it."

The New Dawn ship is still wary of you even after the fight. You watch as they move up to the wreck and carefully extract a single component, you're not what it is, but with that done they back away from the wreck, uninterested in the rest of it. You receive a simple thank you before they leave.

event.name: OG_IRON_PROTOTYPE_DAWN_WIN_INITIATE
event.text: The New Dawn ship is still wary of you even after the fight. You watch as they move up to the wreck and carefully extract a single component, you're not what it is, but with that done they back away from the wreck, uninterested in the rest of it. "Captain, if you ever make it to the New Dawn Forward Operating Base I'll ensure you get through unopposed, try and find me once you're there."
